Petition For Instructions Re: Roe Family Trust Under Prob. 17200, And More Specifically For Order Compelling Attorney For Predecessor Trustee To Trans
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with Hilda Roe (Trustors' Daughter/Beneficiary) and for Gary Frey (Trustee).
Gary Alabaster for and with David Sauers (Respondent).

Ms. Calhoun stated that she requested Trust records held by Mr. Sauers and noted that Mr. Sauers requested a capacity declaration from Dr. Vivian Clayton. Mr. Sauers stated that the holder of the privilege depends on what Trust the Court is questioning, spoke of two other Trusts (Shirley Cole Roe Trust and the Moeller Trust), that David and George were Co-Trustees, provided trust history and history of who became the holder of the privilege, stated that there is no privilege, but spoke of Attorney Client confidentiality, made reference to Business and Professions Code 6068 sub division (e)(1), made reference to the Bing Crosby 2005 case, etc. Ms. Calhoun argues relevance, whether 957 applies, whether or not there has been a waiver on turning over the report, cited several cases, spoke of the work product doctrine, etc. The Court rules that there is no privilege, and orders that the two documents (Mr. Sauers' letter dated August 20, 2008 and e mail of September 15, 2008 referred to in Dr. Clayton's report) be turned over, as well as the documents referred to in paragraph 2 of the relief requested related to the family trust except any documents referred to as work product. The Petition for Instructions Re: Roe Family Trust, etc. is granted in part. A status conference is set for July 11, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Reporter: Carol Haraburda, CSR #8052, 925-360-6065.

Petition For Instructions Re: Roe Family Trust Under Prob. 17200, And More Specifically For Order Compelling Attorney For Predecessor Trustee To Trans
Off Calendar May-02-2018 Continued To May-30-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions Re: Roe Family Trust Under Prob. 17200, And More Specifically For Order Compelling Attorney For Predecessor Trustee To Trans
Off Calendar Mar-21-2018 Continued To May-02-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-17-301464 The George R. Roe Family Trust and PTR-17-301465 The Roe Residence Trust

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for Gary Frey (Trustee/Beneficiary), and for and with Hilda Roe (Beneficiary) of the Family Trust
Gary Alabaster for and with David Sauers (Respondent) in the Family Trust.
Gary Alabaster and David Sauers for Ruth Roe (Trustee) in the Residence Trust.

Ms. Calhoun requested a continuance on the Family Trust stating that she received a late opposition and she was unable to reply. With respect to the Residence Trust, Ms. Calhoun stated that she is willing to accept an informal accounting with bank statements, and that she is no longer requesting removal of Ruth Roe. The Court will not rule/issue an order on these matters. The Parties agree that the handwritten instructions are not an amendment, but are in dispute as to whether or not it is necessary for the Court to issue an order on this matter. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not the handwritten notes/instructions had any effect on the Trust, whether it is a moot point, whether Hilda Roe had any standing to request information on the Residence Trust, whether or not this petition is necessary/appropriate to bring before the Court, whether or not the Trust should pay for Ruth Roe's attorney's fees with respect to opposition to this petition, whether there was any breach of trust/whether the Trustee invaded the principal of the Trust, and whether or not there should be a surcharge against Ruth Roe for petitioner's attorney's fees and costs, etc. The Petition for Instructions re: Roe Family Trust under 17200, etc. is continued to May 2, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Instruction Under Prob. 17200, etc. in the Residence Trust is Taken Under Submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
